mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals a severe deterioration in household income and consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during contraction periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with a declining federal funds rate indicates accommodative monetary policy successfully stimulating consumer demand, which boosts corporate earnings forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 2% monthly while FEDFUNDS falls, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when tested against historical recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding labor demand and rising aggregate income, which supports consumer spending and corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC falls below 0.015 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation accelerates beyond 0.5%, it forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy and erodes real corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in 70% of cases when CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.5%, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return regimes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in housing starts and industrial production signals synchronized strength across residential and manufacturing sectors, which validates broad economic resilience and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST and INDPRO expansion signal predicts negative 20-day returns in 55% of historical instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion, quarterly GDP growth deceleration below 1.5% annualized signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 deceleration fails to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal produces positive returns in 60% of triggered instances across historical late-cycle periods.
